title: pilosella angustifolia subsp. subglacialis
intro: Pilosella angustifolia subsp. subglacialis, also known as a subspecies of the plant commonly referred to as Arctic mouse-ear, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to high-altitude alpine and Arctic regions. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. This plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Pilosella angustifolia subsp. subglacialis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
